title: WA Opens AU$60m Fund for Green Hydrogen and Industrial Decarbonization
content: Western Australiaâ€™s government is cranking up its clean-energy agenda with a fresh AU$60 million funding round through the Investment Attraction Fund. Starting in September 2025, this wave of investment zeros in on green hydrogen, green iron and steel, critical minerals processing and carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS)â€”all designed to diversify the state economy and slash emissions.Premier Roger Cook has put AU$60 million on the table to speed up industrial decarbonization in sectors still hooked on fossil fuels. This round builds on support for lithium battery gigafactories and advanced manufacturing hubsâ€”efforts that not only create skilled jobs but lay the groundwork for a low-carbon industrial powerhouse. Since it kicked off, the Investment Attraction Fund has pumped AU$216.4 million into more than two dozen projects, jump-starting renewable hydrogen, green steel, battery precursors and CCUS trials.
author: Analysts Account
